Urinalysis

Test03-JUL-2025Reference RangeUnit
Glucose [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip9.5 H0 - 5mg/dL
Specific gravity of Urine by Test strip1.0 L1.005 - 1.030{nominal}
pH of Urine by Test strip6.85.0 - 8.0pH
Protein [Mass/volume] in Urine by Test strip3.20 - 20mg/dL
Nitrite [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine nitrite positive
Hemoglobin [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine blood test = +
Leukocyte esterase [Presence] in Urine by Test stripUrine leukocyte test
Leukocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field26.1 H0 - 5/[HPF]
Erythrocytes [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field2.20 - 5/[HPF]
Epithelial cells [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power field3.10 - 10/[HPF]
Casts [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopyUrine microscopy: no casts
Mucus [Presence] in Urine sediment by Light microscopyMucus in urine
Bacteria [#/area] in Urine sediment by Microscopy high power fieldFinding of presence of bacteria

Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The findings indicate a urinary tract infection (UTI) with significant bacteriuria (E. coli >100,000 CFU/mL), positive nitrites, leukocyte esterase, elevated leukocytes, and presence of blood in urine. Glucosuria is noted, which may warrant evaluation for hyperglycemia or diabetes. Low urine specific gravity suggests dilute urine. No casts are seen. Clinical correlation and prompt treatment of UTI are recommended; further assessment for underlying glycemic control is advised.
